artifacts.content:
To: mom.elena.martinez@example.com
From: josh.martinez14@example.com
Subject: Stuff I should probably tell you about my allergies

Mom,

I know we’ve talked about my “regular” allergies (pollen, cats, etc.), but with CAP trips and work getting busier I feel like I should put everything in one place so you’re not surprised if something happens.

First, I’m 100% never taking amoxicillin or any penicillin-type antibiotic again. That ER visit at Methodist Hospital–Stone Oak on March 18, 2023 scared me more than I let on. The hives and my throat tightening up felt like I was breathing through a straw. Dr. Priya Nanduri in the ER was really clear about it, remember? She told us to treat it as a serious allergy from now on. I still feel bad that you only found out about the whole thing when the $640 ER bill came in April and we had to do that payment plan.

Second, the shrimp tacos from the H‑E‑B Bulverde Road hot bar are officially on my “nope” list. Both in June 2024 and again on July 12, my face puffed up and I got that itchy rash. Luis had to send me home three hours early on 7/12 and log it as a safety thing in Workday, which means HR might ask questions. It’s weird because I don’t react to shrimp anywhere else, so maybe it’s something in the seasoning.

Last thing: the vape/meth type smoke stuff really messes me up. The October 6, 2024 incident in Mr. Alvarez’s 5th period English class with the THC vape cloud turned into that whole panic/wheezing episode, and then at Abuela’s at Las Palmas on August 3 when her neighbor was smoking meth in the hallway, my breathing got so bad EMS and the cops came.

I’m not trying to stress you out, just want us both to remember all this when we’re signing anything for CAP trips or school.

Love,
Josh

metadata.subject: Stuff I should probably tell you about my allergies

artifacts.content:
Title: Health / breathing / allergy log – for me

Lately it feels like every random thing sets off my nose or breathing, so I’m writing it down so I don’t forget details.

1) Amoxicillin reaction – March 18, 2023
Methodist Hospital–Stone Oak ER, San Antonio. Took amoxicillin for that STI scare from spring break. Within a day: severe hives + throat tightness. ER doc was Dr. Priya Nanduri. She said it was a serious allergic reaction and I should avoid penicillin-type meds forever. Mom (Elena) didn’t even know I’d gone until the $640 ER bill showed up in April and we set up a payment plan. Still feel guilty about that.

2) Vape / panic combo – October 6, 2024
Mr. Alvarez’s 5th period English, Madison High. Kid secretly vaped THC. I started tight chest, wheezing, shaking. Nurse Ms. Greene called it both an asthma‑like/allergy trigger and an anxiety/panic thing, probably connected to the March 2023 armed lockdown. She called Dad (Carlos) at H‑E‑B. Got referred to counselor to talk about possible PTSD, which I’m still not sure how I feel about.

3) Shrimp tacos at work – June 2024 + July 12, 2024
H‑E‑B Bulverde Road store. Both times after eating hot bar shrimp tacos: mild facial swelling + itchy rash. On 7/12 it was bad enough that Luis Ortega sent me home three hours early and filed a Workday safety incident. HR might follow up.

4) Abuela’s place – August 3, 2024
Las Palmas Senior Living, west side. Neighbor’s meth smoke in hallway. Intense sneezing, watery eyes, short of breath; EMS called, SAPD report.

Remember: bring antihistamines on CAP trips + sit mid‑bus for motion sickness.

metadata.title: Health / breathing / allergy log – for me

artifacts.content:
To: ms.ramirez@madisonhs.edu
From: josh.martinez14@example.com
Subject: Follow-up before parent conference

Hi Ms. Ramirez,

Before you meet with my parents next week, I wanted to give a little context so they don’t get overwhelmed hearing everything at once.

The October 6 incident in Mr. Alvarez’s 5th period English class really shook me more than I expected. When that kid hit the THC vape, it wasn’t just the wheezing and tight chest. It felt like I was right back in the March 2023 lockdown, listening for footsteps in the hallway and wondering if someone was going to come through the door. Ms. Greene in the nurse’s office said she was documenting it as both an allergy/asthma‑type reaction and an anxiety/panic event, and she mentioned possible PTSD. She called my dad at H‑E‑B again, and he sounded freaked out on the phone.

It’s also not the first time smoke has messed with my breathing. On August 3 at my grandma Luisa Rodriguez’s place at Las Palmas Senior Living, her neighbor was apparently smoking meth in the hallway. My allergies went crazy, and I got so short of breath that EMS had to come and SAPD took a report. That’s part of why I get jumpy about any kind of smoke now.

On top of that, there was the amoxicillin ER visit at Methodist Hospital–Stone Oak back on March 18, 2023 with Dr. Priya Nanduri. Different kind of emergency (hives + throat tightness), but it left me kind of paranoid about my body flipping out on me.

I’m still doing fine in most classes (mostly Bs, aiming higher in physics and history), and CAP and work at H‑E‑B actually help me feel more normal. I just wanted you to know where I’m coming from so if I seem on edge, it’s not you.

Thank you for listening and for setting up time with my parents.

– Joshua Martinez, C/SSgt, TX‑351

Event: CAP Encampment – Travel & Medical Prep
Date: 2025-06-21
Time: 15:30–16:15
Location: Kitchen table / home – 4821 Willow Bend Drive
Attendees: Me, Mom, Dad (if off work)

Notes:

Go over everything for summer encampment at the base. Double‑check packing list (boots, blues, PT gear, contacts stuff, allergy meds, motion sickness pills). Talk through all the health/incident stuff so Mom and Dad feel okay about me going.

Things to remember to bring up:

– Amoxicillin allergy: remind them about March 18, 2023 at Methodist Hospital–Stone Oak ER with Dr. Priya Nanduri (severe hives + throat tightness). Make sure I write “no penicillin/amoxicillin” on anything I have to hand over to medical staff.

– Breathing / smoke triggers: explain the October 6, 2024 THC vape episode in Mr. Alvarez’s 5th period English class (wheezing + panic, Ms. Greene called Dad at H‑E‑B, counselor referral). Also mention August 3, 2024 at Abuela Luisa’s place at Las Palmas Senior Living when the neighbor’s meth smoke in the hallway set off sneezing, watery eyes, and shortness of breath bad enough that EMS and SAPD came.

– Food stuff: the shrimp taco reactions at the H‑E‑B Bulverde Road hot bar (June 2024 and July 12, 2024, when Luis sent me home three hours early and logged a Workday safety incident). Not sure if it’s shrimp or something in the store recipe, but probably safest to avoid shellfish at encampment anyway.

– Regular allergies: seasonal pollen (sneezing, itchy/watery eyes, mild congestion) and cat dander. Bring antihistamines.

– Motion sickness: sit mid‑bus, no reading, take meds before long bus ride.

Goal: leave this meeting with Mom/Dad feeling confident and me having a small card with all key info in my wallet.

metadata.title: CAP Encampment – Travel & Medical Prep
metadata.location: 4821 Willow Bend Drive, San Antonio, TX

artifacts.content:
Group chat: "Martinez Cousins" (excerpt)
Date: 2024-08-10

Me: y’all still coming down for Labor Day?

Cousin Ana: maybe, Mom’s worried about the heat + allergies.

Me: lol same. SA in September is just pollen and sweat.

Cousin Diego: didn’t you end up in the ER once from allergies??

Me: kinda. That was March 18 last year at Methodist Hospital–Stone Oak, but it wasn’t just pollen. They’d put me on amoxicillin for… “reasons” over spring break and I broke out in crazy hives + my throat got tight. ER doc, Dr. Nanduri, said never again with penicillin stuff.

Ana: omg dude

Me: worst part was Mom only found out when the $640 ER bill came in April and she was like “WHEN did this happen??”

Diego: 💀💀💀

Me: and now smoke is my enemy too. On 10/6 in Mr. Alvarez’s 5th period English, some idiot hit a THC vape and I started wheezing and full panic mode. Nurse Ms. Greene said it was like asthma + anxiety from that lockdown in March 2023. She called my dad at H‑E‑B and he thought I’d passed out or something.

Ana: that’s rough

Me: then on 8/3 at Abuela Luisa’s place at Las Palmas Senior Living, her neighbor was literally smoking meth in the hallway. My allergies went nuclear, I couldn’t breathe, EMS rolled up and SAPD took a report. I’m low‑key scared to visit her now.

Diego: so we’re doing game night at YOUR house then 😂

Me: yes pls. Grandma can come here, no meth neighbors, just Miguel yelling at FIFA.

To: luis.ortega@heb.com
From: josh.martinez14@example.com
Subject: Quick follow-up on shrimp taco incident + schedule

Hi Luis,

Thanks again for looking out for me on July 12 when my face puffed up after the shrimp tacos from the hot bar. I felt bad that you had to send me home three hours early and log it as a safety incident in Workday, but I get why you did. Just wanted to put in writing that the same thing happened once back in June 2024 on a Friday night shift – milder that time, just itchy rash and a little swelling.

I’ve eaten shrimp at home and at other places without any problem, so I don’t know if it’s the seasoning or cross‑contact with something else at the Bulverde Road store. Either way, I’m going to stay away from shrimp and probably all shellfish while I’m on shift, just in case.

Part of why I’m being extra careful is I’ve already had a couple of scary health things the last few years. In March 2023 I ended up in the Methodist Hospital–Stone Oak ER with a bad reaction to amoxicillin – full‑body hives and my throat getting tight. The ER doctor (Dr. Priya Nanduri) told me to treat it like a serious allergy from now on. Then this past school year I had that episode in Mr. Alvarez’s 5th period English class on October 6 when a kid vaped THC and I started wheezing and freaking out. The school nurse, Ms. Greene, said it was kind of like an asthma/allergy trigger mixed with anxiety from the lockdown we had in March 2023.

I’m totally fine working my regular shifts and can still help out wherever you need me, I just won’t touch the shrimp stuff anymore. If HR needs any info from me about the incident, let me know.

Thanks,
Joshua Martinez

metadata.subject: Quick follow-up on shrimp taco incident + schedule
metadata.location: H‑E‑B Bulverde Road, San Antonio, TX

artifacts.content:
Title: Talking points for visit to Abuela’s place

I want to go see Abuela Luisa more, but I keep stressing about her building at Las Palmas Senior Living. Writing this out so I can talk to Mom and Dad without sounding all over the place.

1. August 3, 2024 incident
Went with Mom and Miguel to drop off groceries. Hallway outside Abuela’s unit smelled like chemical/burnt plastic – later officers said likely meth from the neighbor’s apartment. Within minutes: intense sneezing, watery eyes, and then that short‑of‑breath feeling. Mom called 911, EMS checked my vitals, SAPD took a report about the drug use in the building. I was embarrassed but also legit scared.

2. Not the first breathing scare
Back on October 6, 2024 in Mr. Alvarez’s 5th period English at Madison, a classmate secretly vaped THC. I started wheezing and panicking. Nurse Ms. Greene said the vape cloud probably triggered an asthma‑like reaction and also anxiety from the March 2023 armed lockdown. She called Dad at H‑E‑B and referred me to the counselor.

3. Plus the whole amoxicillin ER thing
March 18, 2023 – Methodist Hospital–Stone Oak ER with Dr. Priya Nanduri. Severe hives and throat tightness after starting amoxicillin. Mom only found out when the $640 bill came in April and we had to set up a payment plan.

4. What I want
– Visit Abuela more, but maybe meet her at our house or a park until management really fixes the neighbor issue.
– Ask Mom if we can keep an inhaler or at least antihistamines handy when we go.
– Make sure everyone takes the smoke stuff seriously, not just as “Josh being dramatic.”

metadata.title: Talking points for visit to Abuela’s place
